title: Artanthe acutifolia
intro: Artanthe acutifolia, also known as the wild passionflower, is a climbing vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Artanthe acutifolia include its potential to aid in anxiety relief, insomnia, and restlessness, due in part to its sedative and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Artanthe acutifolia is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.

The Achariaceae family is a small group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical regions. Artanthe acutifolia is one of the few species within the Achariaceae family that has been extensively studied. The classification of Artanthe acutifolia reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
